div.p: Record Number 48 Story-song and Stealing Dance 45
Women sing with the men until the
turn of the song, then they swing
and the women don't sing no more;
Words of the Rabbit Dance Song
hą̈ą̈ą̈ hû · i hą̈ą̈ą̈ hûi :|| 2 yô
See Table of Contents of Book IV for page number notes on the
way this song goes with the dancing.
In the Rabbit Dance the man and the woman sing.
at the end of each rendition
This fast part does not come
to occur at the beginning of the
song, I think, but it does not
come out at all on the record.
cu´ruku = he blows (the whistle); he smokes
The old-time Tunica Indians used to make a
Sesostrie never saw one of these.
They had several holes on the side
(Sesostrie doesn't know how many) which used
According to Sesostrie big cane is about the
size of a semicircle made with his
thumb and forefinger stretched out but
They were 2½ feet long and maybe more,
Sesostrie doesn't know what the social function
They used to use bones (probably wolf
and he doesn't know much about them.
drumming are better on this record.
of the drumming is quite distinct.
has quite a few low notes in it which the
informant could not bring out clearly and
these, of course, would not record.
The first rendition of the song rendition
of the song starts at 14 on the Dictaphone Record.
The chorus of this song sounds
as if it might be influenced by negro
The two renditions were not taken
right together, but there is a break between them.
The song should be sung four times straight
Words of the " Coon " Dance Song
||: ||: hi-ni hyu-wi-k'u-na / yu-wi-k'u-na :|| 3 wi || ya hi-ya :|| 2
Words of the " Coon " Dance Song (Continued)
kα-nα wi-o wi · hα ·/ hi · hα · wi-hα
(*** Sometimes these two lines are reversed.)
Number of hu-ya-ne before na varies, sometimes more than 3, sometimes less)
Words of the " Coon " Dance Song (Continued)
||: we · ha · hyo · wa-nu / ha-nu hu-ya-ne :|| 3 or 4
Just the leader sings for the
In this dance the men and women
All in all this is a fairly good record.
Pitch of A is recorded at 47.
From 48 to the end of the record,
past 55, approximately 58, another rendition of the
song is given with the singing closer in
the song is sung four times with the
drumming and singing both more or less
however, with the singing in the mouthpiece,
Words of the Duck Dance Song
||: yα-kə-wî yo yô · (" answer " by the men ") :|| 2
Words of the Duck Dance Song (Continued)
||: yô hî yä-wä / hä-wä hi-yα hi-yα
yô hı̂ yä-wä / hä-wä hi-yα hi-yα
Just the leader sings for the Duck Dance;
In one place there is some " answering "
Number of times sung here is 3 or 4, neither of us remembers
how many; however, it is supposed
to be sung 4 times just like the previous
There is a little drumming without
singing (which can scarcely be heard) at the beginning of the record (1-6).
This is due to the fact that the informant could not
remember how the song started and so he
just kept drumming trying to remember.
However, he finally asked me to stop the
machine until he could think of it.
the song and drumming as should
singing is done closer into the
at the end of the record.
||: hı-ko-we-no ye hı-ko-we-no ye /(h conn) e
hı-ko-we-no ye ho yą hä-na-he / ha-na-he :|| 2
hi-hu / we-hu ya · ne · hą̈ / yą̈ huwe to-na he
* Beginning here the leader sings alone; before that
the men and women both sing.
renditions are with drum and singing both
pitch A is recorded at the end of the record.
The machine was running on Slow.
The song is supposed to be sung
wa-hi-ya hą̈' hi ya hu ya hą̈' :||
hi ya hu ya hą̈' / ha' :|| 4 [ yô ·]
Variation at beginning ||: hi-ya nıtα hu hi-ya nı-ta :|| 3
Just the drummer sings the Bear Dance
is close to the mouthpiece, but the drumming
Pitch of A recorded at the end.
The song is to be sung four times.
The informant put both parts on the record.*
Possibly later on I can get 2
people in Marksville to sing together.
of these animal songs is supposed to
imitate that animal in its words.
* The informant put most of the answering part on the record,
Words of the Alligator Dance Song
Leader : ||: hα-ni-he nu wi · he hi
All the men / Everybody whooping : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: hα-ni-he nu wi · he hi
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: hα-ni-he nu wi · he hi
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Although the wowrds are correct, the
way the " answering " goes is not correctly
Leader : ||: yą-yo (men) (but not leader) hi hâ :|| 12 (?)
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: 'ų-ho-ho (men, but not leader) hi ha :|| 10
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: yą-yo (men, but not leader) hi hâ :||
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: 'ų-ho-ho (men, but not leader) hi ha :||
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Leader : ||: yą-yo (men, but not leader) hi hâ :||
All the men / Everybody : yɛ yɛ yɛ yɛ 'û ·
Just the leader and the men sing the
Pitch of A is recorded in the beginning of
blurred sometimes on this record, possibly
because he was singing too loud too close in
Words of the After-midnight Dance Song
wi-hi yä :|| 10 [ yα-hâ · yä̂ ·] yâ · yä̂
hi-wą yo · hi-wą yo · hi-wą yo
Both men and women sing the
pitch for Records Number 45 and Number 46 recorded
at the beginning of Record Number 46.
from Number 46 on n is one rendition of the
the pewter ice-pail as drum whereas
record number 38 was made with a box as drum
last syllable 'yo' did not get on n record.
The different parts of the song are not always
repeated the same number of times.
The leader and the men sing this song.
The leader sings the words indicated in
blue in kand the men " answer " the
parts are not sung at the same
time, but first the leader sings and
Pitch A at the beginning of the record.
Words of the Quail Dance Song
The men and women both sing, except for
the (h)yô · which is sung by the leader alone.
Transferred onto an aluminum disc [ February 28, 1936 ].
The whole record is not there, but it goes to Dictate # 47
This song is sung in the story
Pitch of A at the end of the song.
After that is another pitch A which
goes for the next song on the record.
The first part of this on this record (14-16) is bad because the dictate was run over it again
Sam says you can sing this song
this song four times, you just go
Words of the Stealing Dance Song
||: hα-ne ··/(h conn) yα-ne ··/ yα-ne ·· hα-ne /(h conn) yα-ne / yα-ne ku-we ·/ ku-wi · :|| 2
||: yo-hα̨ / ho-hα̨ he ·/ hi · (2nd time and 4th) hu-ya 'a-ne · :|| 4 or 2
Sam says this song sounds prettier
The men and women both sing
In this dance the men are in one line
and the women are in another.
the others, then they stop the dance.
However, the dance can go on and on.
This record does not contain all of the
round dance, but it contains several
Sam says they can sing all
night, and keep on singing different
Sam says that only the men sing
(See next page for beginning of words)
Words of the Round Dance Song
There is " answering " in this song, too
it is 3 times on the record.
ye / he ye / he ye / he ye / he
and the recording, see page 71 ff.)
The leader's part is represented by blue ink
and the " answering " part by red ink.
The women don't sing this song,
Each section is two rounds; then they whoop.
||: hi-yo-ha hi-yą-ha :|| several times :|| a few times
3rd Section : ||: hyo hα̨-hi-yą̈ hyo hα̨-hi-yą̈ :|| many times
4th Section : ||: hyä-na / ho-na hi-yo :|| many times
and not done by the singer.
5th Section : ||: hyu wa-ne-hą hyu wa-ne-hą :|| many times
6th Section : ||: * hyα-nen-hu / hyα-ne-hu hi-ya hyα-nen-hu / hyα-ne-hu hi-ya :|| many times
7th Section : ||: ha-na / ha · -na (sometimes) ti-yo hı̂' hä̂' :|| several times
2 times ||: ha-na te-hu-he hı̂' ** hä̂' :|| a few times
||: ha-na ti-yo hı̂' hä̂' :|| several times
For the most part, this is a good record.
From the 8th through he 13th section (1-39), the
informant went straight on without stopping.
However, at the end of the 13th section, he
was unable the remember the 14th one right
away, so the machine was stopped.
pitch (A) [ at 40 ] of those sections was recorded.
As the speed of sections 14 and 15 was slightly
different from the speed of the preceding
sections, an attempt was made to put the pitch
(A) in for those sections immediately after the
pitch of the preceding sections, but, unfortunately,
this attempt resulted in the pitch interfering
with the first of the singing of the 14th section.
In the case of the fifteenth section, the informant
had to stop to remember it, and so there
is a break between the 14th and 15th sections
and the informant started singing the 15th
section an instant before dictate was turned on.
there was not quite enough room on the
record for all of the 15th section.
it will be advisable to retake the 14th and
Words of the Round Dance Song
||: hi-yą hyu-we ha · :|| about 3 :|| a few times
9th Section : ||: hyu hα-na we hwî · (allegro)/ wi-hî · (lento) ha · :||
||: hi-yą hyu-we hą hwı̂ ·/ wi-hı̂ · ha · :||
||: hyu hα-na we · hwı̂ ·/ wi-hi · ha · :||
* You can whoop any way you want to.
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
11th Section : ||: we-he'-ya /(h conn) e-he'-ya hi-yą we · -he · -ya ·/(h conn) e · -he · -yą · :||
12th Section : ||: hu-ya wi-ho ha-yą ·* :|| several times
13th Section : ||: hı-kα-na yu-wä yo-hä / hyo-hä /(h conn) o-hä :||
Pitch of A for preceding sections at Dictate 40
Pitch of A for succeeding sections at Dictate 42
(This 2nd pitch interferes with beginning of next section on record.)
* Informant often begins the singing with the " answer ".
In this case, the singing goes on just the same as indicated.
before the " whoop " is always the " answer ".
On Record Number 21, beginning at 42, informant gets
mixed up in the order of the sections.
14th Section : ||: hyu we-hi-yą / we-hi- (h conn) ą we · -hi · -ha ·/ we · -hi · -ya · :||
a break in the singing - the next three sections
||: he · -ya · hα-na-we · :|| several times :|| a few times
Informant started singing, but then stopped
deciding he had sung that one before.
the same as the preceding one.
21st Section : ||: he-ya ni-na hi-yo-ha :|| many times
The beginning of the 22nd Section - but
there was not room for all of it.
Pitch of A recorded at 0-1 for both
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
* This little part, which evidently comes in only once, Sesostrie calls " a little air."
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
29th Section : ||: ya-ni / wa-hi hyo hą hi-yę
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
30th Section : ||: ||: hyo hą he · wą-he :|| 3 Finis
hi-ya / hi-yo hą ha · :|| several times
hi-ya / hi-yo hą ha · :|| several times :|| a few times
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
(One of the melodies of the " answer ")
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
Pitch the same sa Record Number 24
Words of the Round Dance Song (Continued)
This is the next to the last section and is known
as the Snake Dance, hipunara or
Words of the Round Dance Song (continued)
36th section : This is the same as the first
songs are always sung in the same order.
4-29 All 3 parts - leader in the horn
The accompanists are not overly familiar
Words of the Alleigator Dance Song
Leader ||: ||: hα-ni-he nu wi · he hi :||
Chorus of men ||: yą-yo (singer) hî hâ (3rd singer) :||
changed positions with relation to the
Just how these two parts go together, I
to make a record with two people singing.
I do not know whether the rhythm
of the two parts is the same or not, but
at any rate the two parts are sung
about 24-27 spoiled by running over on n dictate
same singers carrying same parts as in Record
a line equals a whole step (i.
semi-tone from line to space and from space to line
'u´wi ni´ku ta´l'ukinic 'u´wa = he said he was going to town
'u´wį pro ni´ku se´hiti n ta´l'ukinic n 'u´wak'ahtca
ra´ct'ä yo´yą ya´'ahkini = I'm working hard
semitones for distance between lines and spaces
mą´'ą ra´ct'ä ya´wihkį = (you m.) work hard!
'i´mą wima´ką ra´ct'ä ya´wihkį = I ask you: you working hard
hä´mą pro ra´ct'ä adv yo´yą yahihkį v.I
The boys know this song well.
3-10 mis-try - the boys made a mistake
2nd section one of the boys came in
too soon a few times, but the other
sections 31, 32, 33, 34, 35 and 1 which is the last as well as first song
Record to be retaken to get it stronger
seems to be a good record
He beats his heel at every 2nd drum beat,
Starts somewhat back from beginning of
See words for beginning of song,
starts with what was formerly 10th section
Part of the Sun-Dance song - as
* Sometimes three ča-nis and sometimes two
Sesostrie thinks the words are Tunica, and
tries to make it hali n čuʔonta v.I -
Sam has heard a " turtle dance " but it's
been so long ago that he has forgotten it.
The last song of the Round Dance is
Sometimes the Round Dance is called
to get them started to go to war.
appears that they would dance all night
and then go to war the next day.
tɔ´hkatoh = cercelle: a small duck that sings all night, tuwi˳s in Chitimacha
